nodes.title: Continuity is the test
nodes.text:
The test is not whether the animation feels cinematic in isolation. It is whether someone can name
the selected thought before and after the movement, predict where a neighboring thought went, and
continue the task without searching from the beginning. [[Quiet Constellations]] applies this rule
to the visual system, and [[Wayfinding Study]] supplies observable navigation tasks. Reduced-motion
behavior should preserve the same information with shorter transitions and no ambient drift.
nodes.summary: The test is not whether the animation feels cinematic in isolation. It is whether someone can name the selected thought before and after the movement, predict where a neighboring thought went, and…

nodes.title: Returning to unfinished ideas
nodes.text:
A fictional design studio is testing whether a spatial map helps people return to an unfinished
idea faster than a folder tree. The study begins with three ordinary tasks: locate an earlier
decision, explain why two notes are related, and resume a thread after a short interruption. The
map should reveal context gradually instead of presenting every label at once.
nodes.summary: A fictional design studio is testing whether a spatial map helps people return to an unfinished idea faster than a folder tree. The study begins with three ordinary tasks: locate an earlier…

nodes.title: Gesture states need release
nodes.text:
The interaction needs explicit begin, confirm, hold, and release states. [[Gesture Thresholds]] now
captures that model, and [[Prototype Review]] will treat repeated activation as a blocking defect.
This entry follows [[Field Note — 14 April 2026]] so the eventual temporal topology has a small but
meaningful journal sequence to connect.
nodes.summary: The interaction needs explicit begin, confirm, hold, and release states. [[Gesture Thresholds]] now captures that model, and [[Prototype Review]] will treat repeated activation as a blocking defect.…

nodes.title: Constellations before filing
nodes.text:
A memory observatory is a place for looking across thoughts rather than filing them away. Notes
appear as a quiet constellation whose lines have inspectable reasons: an explicit reference, a
shared topic, a nearby date, or a structural relationship inside one document. The interface does
not claim to interpret a person's mind; it offers alternative arrangements of material they already
wrote.
nodes.summary: A memory observatory is a place for looking across thoughts rather than filing them away. Notes appear as a quiet constellation whose lines have inspectable reasons: an explicit reference, a shared…

nodes.title: Feedback breaks the stalemate
nodes.text:
In a fictional afternoon trial, the first participant paused at the overview because every point
seemed equally important. Once the pointer caused a nearby title and two strong relationships to
brighten, the task became obvious. The participant moved toward the response before reading the
instruction a second time.
nodes.summary: In a fictional afternoon trial, the first participant paused at the overview because every point seemed equally important. Once the pointer caused a nearby title and two strong relationships to…

nodes.title: Pinch is a transition
nodes.text:
A pinch should be treated as a short state transition, not a single-frame distance check. The
fictional prototype enters a candidate state when thumb and index fingertips approach, confirms
selection only after several stable frames, and requires a wider release distance before another
pinch can begin. That hysteresis prevents noisy landmarks from producing repeated clicks.
nodes.summary: A pinch should be treated as a short state transition, not a single-frame distance check. The fictional prototype enters a candidate state when thumb and index fingertips approach, confirms…

nodes.title: Thresholds across depth
nodes.text:
Thresholds should be recorded in normalized hand coordinates and tested across near, middle, and
far camera positions. False activations matter more than shaving a few milliseconds from selection,
but excessive dwell makes the graph feel unresponsive. Results feed into [[Prototype Review]], and
the informal observation in [[Field Note — 24 April 2026]] provides a scenario for replaying a slow,
deliberate pinch followed by an open-palm return.
nodes.summary: Thresholds should be recorded in normalized hand coordinates and tested across near, middle, and far camera positions. False activations matter more than shaving a few milliseconds from selection,…

nodes.title: Gestures need rest states
nodes.text:
The fictional gesture rehearsal worked best when the participant could rest between actions. A
slow pinch selected the intended thought, but keeping the fingers together while reading caused the
early classifier to fire twice. An open palm was easier to recognize after the hand moved back to a
neutral distance from the camera.
nodes.summary: The fictional gesture rehearsal worked best when the participant could rest between actions. A slow pinch selected the intended thought, but keeping the fingers together while reading caused the…

nodes.title: Topology without replacement
nodes.text:
Semantic, cluster, temporal, and force layouts are different explanations of the same material.
Their transitions should behave like a change of viewpoint, not a replacement scene. The continuity
rule comes from [[Legibility in Motion]], while [[Memory Observatory]] supplies the broader mood.
The observation in [[Field Note — 14 April 2026]] is a reminder that calm pacing still needs a
visible response the instant the user acts.
nodes.summary: Semantic, cluster, temporal, and force layouts are different explanations of the same material. Their transitions should behave like a change of viewpoint, not a replacement scene. The continuity…

nodes.title: Identity survives motion
nodes.text:
Motion is useful when it preserves an object's identity across a change. A node that travels from
one topology to another should remain recognizable through position, brightness, and a continuous
path. If labels flicker, edges redraw abruptly, or the camera moves at a competing speed, the viewer
must solve the interface again after every transition.
nodes.summary: Motion is useful when it preserves an object's identity across a change. A node that travels from one topology to another should remain recognizable through position, brightness, and a continuous…

nodes.title: Contrast without clutter
nodes.text:
The graph should feel spacious even when many relationships exist. Most edges begin thin and dim;
labels wait until attention approaches; selected neighborhoods gain contrast without turning every
other thought invisible. A dark field is useful only when it supports reading, so bloom, particles,
and depth effects remain subordinate to node identity.
nodes.summary: The graph should feel spacious even when many relationships exist. Most edges begin thin and dim; labels wait until attention approaches; selected neighborhoods gain contrast without turning every…

nodes.title: Orientation before action
nodes.text:
The first view should invite orientation before action. Pointing reveals a title, selection gathers
related thoughts, and traversal carries the camera along one explainable edge. The restrained visual
tone is described in [[Quiet Constellations]]. [[Wayfinding Study]] turns this premise into tasks
that can fail clearly, keeping the idea grounded in navigation rather than spectacle.
nodes.summary: The first view should invite orientation before action. Pointing reveals a title, selection gathers related thoughts, and traversal carries the camera along one explainable edge. The restrained…

nodes.title: Observable wayfinding tasks
nodes.text:
Each participant first explores [[Memory Observatory]] with a mouse, then repeats the task while
the graph changes between semantic and temporal arrangements. Observers record hesitation,
backtracking, and accidental selections rather than asking whether the interface merely looks
interesting. [[Legibility in Motion]] defines the visual checks, while [[Prototype Review]] turns
the observations into the next build decisions.
nodes.summary: Each participant first explores [[Memory Observatory]] with a mouse, then repeats the task while the graph changes between semantic and temporal arrangements. Observers record hesitation,…

nodes.title: Calm still acknowledges action
nodes.text:
Calm does not mean withholding all feedback. The graph needs a small immediate acknowledgment,
followed by slower contextual motion. That sequence belongs in [[Quiet Constellations]] and should
be measured through [[Wayfinding Study]]. The next session will separate hover feedback from
selection feedback so an exploratory point never looks like a committed navigation step.
nodes.summary: Calm does not mean withholding all feedback. The graph needs a small immediate acknowledgment, followed by slower contextual motion. That sequence belongs in [[Quiet Constellations]] and should be…

nodes.title: Context held through traversal
nodes.text:
The fictional review group could follow highlighted relationships when the selected thought stayed
bright and the surrounding graph became quieter. They understood the [demo route](/demo) without a
tour once the pointer, selection, and return actions used the same visual language. The strongest
moment was moving from [[Wayfinding Study]] into a related note without losing the prior location.
nodes.summary: The fictional review group could follow highlighted relationships when the selected thought stayed bright and the surrounding graph became quieter. They understood the demo route without a tour once…

nodes.title: Slower intent, clearer motion
nodes.text:
Selection still felt too eager near dense clusters, and fast topology changes made labels difficult
to reacquire. The next pass will use the measured boundaries in [[Gesture Thresholds]] and the
motion rules in [[Quiet Constellations]]. A successful revision should reduce accidental activation
without making intentional traversal feel delayed or mechanical.
nodes.summary: Selection still felt too eager near dense clusters, and fast topology changes made labels difficult to reacquire. The next pass will use the measured boundaries in [[Gesture Thresholds]] and the…
